basics.name: Mattia Asti
basics.label: Software Engineer
basics.summary: I'm a self-taught software engineer with over 14 years of experience currently based in London, UK. I work best with TypeScript across the full stack while specializing in the frontend, where I've been using React for years. These days, a lot of my work is AI-assisted, and I've used Claude Code heavily over the past year. I'm the type of engineer who thrives at the intersection of product and code; I genuinely enjoy talking to users, understanding their problems, and shaping solutions that address real needs. I'm a big fan of home automation and love finding new ways to use my collection of Raspberry Pis.

work.name: Apple
work.summary: I'm part of Apple Services Engineering where I work on internal tooling allowing us to scale our localization across 175 countries and over 50 languages.
highlights: Productionized a novel agentic workflow for editorial content generation, replacing an opaque AI "black box" with a polished UI that lets internal users see and steer the agent at every step.
Built an internal remote caching service written in Rust that is used by multiple teams, resulting in over 6,000 hours saved in the first year.
Led extensive work to optimize a monorepo of 9 web apps and shared components with state-of-the-art tooling, reducing CI pipeline times from 30 minutes down to ~3 minutes.
Lead AI adoption within my organization, establishing best practices for effective use, driving internal discussions on AI workflows, and presenting findings at engineering forums.
Actively contribute to the internal frontend community by authoring shared libraries and best-practice guides used company-wide. My internal Playwright Docker image has surpassed 100k downloads.
Partner with directors of engineering on internal community initiatives, organizing high-profile events including an EMEA frontend conference and an internal talk featuring the creator of Bun.

work.name: tru.ID (IDlayr)
work.summary: tru.ID is an early-stage startup transforming authentication with SIM security. I was the owner of the frontend for the user-facing products. We used React with Next.js and leveraged the Vercel serverless platform
highlights: Built the Developer Portal which included: public docs, code samples and a console dashboard.
Extensive work on the authentication layer including OIDC integrations and e2e tests with Playwright.
Led the development of the design system and React component library with a strict focus on a11y.

work.name: 11:FS
work.summary: 11:FS is a leading Fintech consultancy. I built their authentication layer with Auth0, including the architecture of a mobile passwordless login. I was one of the engineers responsible for running demos to our existing and potential clients, including top-tier banks.

work.name: TES Global
work.summary: TES was one of the largest professional networks in the world with 8M+ teachers. I worked on a significant refactoring of a payment gateway used in the resources marketplace, the highest-traffic part of the website with ~500k daily views.

work.name: Hextech
work.summary: Started as a side project with friends working in our spare time during evenings. Over the years we built 2 mobile apps, an e-commerce, and tried to launch a new startup to help kids learn about financial education. Learned a lot from our failures.

work.name: TechHub
work.summary: TechHub was the leading co-working space in London. I've integrated our backend systems with different payment gateways and dealt with localization after launching 5 international locations. I also built the internal CRM, website, and a mobile app for the members.
